The default interactive shell is now zsh.
To update your account to use zsh, please run `chsh -s /bin/zsh`.
For more details, please visit https://support.apple.com/kb/HT208050.
(base) Lexuss-MacBook-Pro:DevFest 25 chenlingzhi$ npm run dev
npm error Missing script: "dev"
npm error
npm error To see a list of scripts, run:
npm error   npm run
npm error A complete log of this run can be found in: /Users/chenlingzhi/.npm/_logs/2025-02-07T00_00_08_274Z-debug-0.log

The issue is that npm cannot find a "dev" script in your package.json.

To fix it:

1. Open package.json and check "scripts"
2. Ensure there is a dev script, e.g.
   "dev": "node server.js"
3. If not present, add it
4. Or run npm run to see available scripts

Also make sure you're in the correct project directory where package.json exists.

npm run shows:
dev -> node backend/server.js

but running npm run dev gives:

Error: listen EADDRINUSE :::5000

This error means port 5000 is already in use.

Fix options:

1. Find process using port 5000:
   lsof -i :5000

2. Kill it:
   kill -9 <PID>

3. Or use:
   npx kill-port 5000

4. Or change your server port in backend/server.js:
   const PORT = 5001;

Then restart npm run dev.

Also ensure only one app.listen(5000) exists.
Process on port 5000 killed but still getting EADDRINUSE error

If killing the process didn’t work, try:

1. Confirm again:
   lsof -i :5000
   or
   sudo lsof -i tcp:5000

2. Use force tool:
   npx kill-port 5000

3. Check for duplicate server instances in code:
   - only one app.listen(5000)

4. Try different port:
   PORT=5001

5. Restart machine if zombie process persists

Most likely something is still bound to :::5000 or server is being started twice.
